artifact_title: CloudOps Agentic AI powered by Amazon Bedrock AgentCore

findings.description: Wildcard resource ('*') combined with CloudTrail actions. While CloudTrail LookupEvents requires wildcard resources, actions like GetTrailStatus, DescribeTrails, GetEventSelectors, and ListTrails could potentially be scoped to specific trail ARNs if the trails are known at deployment time.
findings.recommendation: If the CloudTrail trails are known at deployment time, scope the GetTrailStatus, DescribeTrails, and GetEventSelectors actions to specific trail ARN patterns. Keep LookupEvents and ListTrails with Resource: '*' and add an inline comment explaining these API operations require wildcard resources by design.

findings.description: Wildcard resource ('*') combined with extensive read-only describe actions across multiple services (EKS, RDS, OpenSearch, ElastiCache, MSK, EC2). While these are read-only actions, the wildcard scope grants visibility into all resources across these services account-wide.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate whether the inventory MCP server needs account-wide visibility or if it can be scoped to specific regions, resource tags, or naming patterns. If account-wide read access is genuinely required for the inventory functionality, add an inline comment explaining that these read-only Describe/List actions do not support resource-level scoping and are required for cross-service inventory discovery. Consider whether the scope can be limited by adding Condition blocks restricting to specific regions or resource tags.

findings.description: Wildcard resource ('*') combined with service-scoped wildcard actions 'cloudwatch:*' and 'logs:*'. This grants full CloudWatch and CloudWatch Logs permissions across all resources in the account, including write and delete capabilities.
findings.recommendation: Replace the service-scoped wildcards with specific actions needed for the CloudWatch MCP server functionality. For read-only operations, use actions like cloudwatch:DescribeAlarms, cloudwatch:GetMetricData, logs:DescribeLogGroups, logs:FilterLogEvents. If write operations are needed, scope them to specific log group ARN patterns rather than all resources.

findings.description: Wildcard resource pattern for AgentCore memory permissions. The resource ARN uses 'arn:aws:bedrock-agentcore:${this.region}:${this.account}:memory/*' which grants access to all memory resources in the account, not just the specific memory created by this stack.
findings.recommendation: Scope the memory permissions to the specific memory resource created by this stack. The memory ID is available as memory.memoryId (line 135), so construct a specific ARN like 'arn:aws:bedrock-agentcore:${this.region}:${this.account}:memory/${memory.memoryId}' instead of using the wildcard pattern.

findings.description: MCP Runtime configured with NetworkMode: 'PUBLIC' exposes the service directly to the internet. Found: 'NetworkConfiguration: { NetworkMode: 'PUBLIC' }' on Billing MCP Runtime (line 182), Pricing MCP Runtime (line 231), CloudWatch MCP Runtime (line 302), CloudTrail MCP Runtime (line 375), and Inventory MCP Runtime (line 492). While JWT authorization is configured, public network exposure increases the attack surface and violates defense-in-depth principles.
findings.recommendation: Change NetworkMode to PRIVATE and place the runtimes in a VPC with private subnets. Access should be routed through an Application Load Balancer or API Gateway in public subnets, which then forwards authenticated requests to the private runtimes. This provides network-level isolation in addition to application-level JWT authentication. Update the NetworkConfiguration to specify VPC configuration with private subnet IDs and security groups that restrict inbound traffic to only the load balancer or API Gateway.

findings.description: Package 'semver' version 6.3.1 has a known high-severity vulnerability (CVE-2022-25883, CVSS 7.5). This Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) vulnerability in the 'new Range' function can cause denial of service when processing untrusted user data. The vulnerability affects all versions before 7.5.2 on the 7.x branch and before 6.3.1 on the 6.x branch.
findings.recommendation: Update the semver package to version 7.5.2 or later. Run 'npm update semver' or modify package.json to require semver version 7.5.2 or higher, then regenerate package-lock.json with 'npm install'.

findings.description: Wildcard resource ('*') combined with service-scoped wildcard actions including 'ce:*', 'budgets:*', 'compute-optimizer:*', 'cost-optimization-hub:*'. While Cost Explorer and Budgets APIs require wildcard resources, the policy grants excessive permissions beyond what's needed for a billing MCP server (e.g., ec2:DescribeInstances, lambda:ListFunctions, ecs:ListServices are not billing-specific).
findings.recommendation: Split this policy into two statements: (1) Cost Explorer and Budgets actions with Resource: '*' and a comment explaining these services require wildcard resources, and (2) EC2/Lambda/ECS describe actions scoped to specific resource ARNs or removed if not needed for the billing functionality. Review whether compute-optimizer:* and cost-optimization-hub:* wildcards are necessary or can be restricted to specific actions.
